question_text: A solar panel system converts sunlight into electrical energy with an efficiency of 18%. On a sunny day, 4000 kJ of solar energy hits the panels. How much electrical energy is generated, and what happens to the remaining energy?
correct_answer: Electrical energy generated: 720 kJ. Remaining energy (3280 kJ) is mostly converted to heat through reflection and absorption in materials.
explanation: Useful electrical energy = 18% × 4000 kJ = 720 kJ. The remaining 82% (3280 kJ) is lost mainly as heat due to reflection from the panel surface and heating of the panel materials themselves. This is why solar panels get warm in sunlight.
